record.title: Lolah Benge Tribble, 1973-07-23, Interview - MP3 Recording
record.subject: Latah County -- Idaho
record.origin: Dick Benge's sister; Hershiel Tribble's wife

point.title: Moving to Idaho from Nebraska; fight with brother hiding in an outhouse
point.synopsis: Move to Idaho a result of letters from Nebraska friends who'd moved out earlier. Fight in Nebraska with brother Dick, hiding in an outhouse.

point.title: Renting a boxcar; train ride with three families; father hit his head
point.synopsis: Parents rented a boxcar to carry belongings out. Train ride with three families. Father lost his sense of balance and hit his head.

point.title: Arrival in Princeton; moving out 30 days later
point.synopsis: Arrival in Princeton. Moved out 30 days later.

point.title: Walking to school; seeing a bear; shocking hay; Mother's day
point.synopsis: Walking to school. Seeing a bear near gate. Lolah Tribble plowed with a foot burner and shocked hay with her mother. Mother's day, did things the hard way.

point.title: Going to Moscow the first time
point.synopsis: Five years after arriving in Idaho, she went to Moscow for the first time to buy a winter coat.

point.title: Father's rules at dances; honeymoon in Spokane
point.synopsis: Boys went to girls' home to court. Father's rules at dances: dance with everyone; don't leave the dance hall. Honeymoon in Spokane.

point.title: Hershiel worked in the woods
point.synopsis: Hershiel worked in woods some after marriage.

point.title: Hershiel logging; cooking for men at logging camps; time away from parents
point.synopsis: Stayed near Collins once when Hershiel logged. Cooked for men once at logging camp near home. Only times away from parents.

point.title: Got used to accidents in the woods
point.synopsis: Didn't worry about accidents in the woods. You just got used to it.

point.title: Nicknames; Hershiel; Uncle Shorty
point.synopsis: Nicknames. Hershiel. Uncle Shorty. Dick. Babe (her own).

point.title: Moonshining; moonshine from Onaway called day-glow red
point.synopsis: Moonshining. One still just up Hatter Creek. Some moonshine from Onaway called day-glow red.

point.title: Trip to Princeton; Hershiel built school for their children; teachers who taught there
point.synopsis: Half day trip to Princeton. Hershiel Tribble and others built a school for their children. Lolah Tribble describes teachers who taught there.

point.title: Scared of the first man teacher she had; teacher who went to dances with the kids
point.synopsis: Lolah scared of the first man teacher she had when moved to Princeton. Next man teacher was boss in the classroom, but would go to dances with the kids.

point.title: Neighbors were her closest friends
point.synopsis: Nearby neighbors were Lolah Tribble's closest friends. Don't communicate like that now.

record.description: Teenage years: dances, teachers, work. Settling in Idaho. Neighborhood closeness. 7-23-73 1 hr LS
record.transcript: Download the PDF Index No transcript available.
record.rights: Digital format copyright: University of Idaho Library. Original Copyright: Latah County Historical Society
